name: Subtitle OCR
description: Converts embedded image-based subtitle tracks (VobSub/DVD and PGS/Blu-ray) to external SRT or ASS files using nOCR pattern matching. Pure managed code: no Tesseract and no native dependencies. The .nocr database format is interchange-compatible with Subtitle Edit, so databases trained or corrected in its GUI drop straight in. A scheduled task scans your Movie and Episode libraries and writes a subtitle file beside each image-based track. OCR text is optionally spell-checked with Hunspell and corrected with Subtitle Edit's OCR fix lists, both fetched on demand.
overview: OCR image-based DVD and Blu-ray subtitles to SRT or ASS

versions.changelog: The dashboard menu carries both plugin pages: "Subtitle OCR" for the settings and "Extracted Subtitles" for the list of extracted files, which also has a "Reprocess all" button that starts the reprocess task, shows its progress, and reloads the list when it finishes, plus a gear button that opens the settings. The plugin's Settings button opens the settings page.

Fixes 10.11.3.1 stranding you on the extracted-files list: opening it from the plugin settings left no way back to the settings page. The list is now reached from its "Extracted Subtitles" entry in the dashboard menu only, and the settings page no longer links to it.

The list of extracted subtitle files is now a dashboard page of its own, in the main menu, instead of a pop-up inside the plugin settings; the settings button still opens it. The page gained a "Reprocess all" button that starts the reprocess task directly, shows its progress, and reloads the list when it finishes, so re-OCRing everything with the current settings no longer means a trip to Scheduled Tasks.

Output format is now selectable: SRT, ASS, or Auto, which decides per track and writes ASS only when a cue sits away from the bottom of the screen (PGS carries the position), so an ordinary track keeps the universally supported format. OCR text is spell-checked with Hunspell, protecting Title-case proper nouns and words drawn from the item's Jellyfin metadata (title, overview, series, cast and character names) so show and character names survive. Subtitle Edit OCR fix replace lists are applied when present; dictionaries and fix lists download on demand and re-download once older than a configurable age. A track with an undetermined language now has one detected from its OCR text and is named accordingly, and the track title, forced, hearing-impaired, and commentary flags appear in the file name so commentary tracks are identifiable. A hearing-impaired track is recognized from its own text when the source flags nothing, which is the norm for a remuxed disc. New OCR corrections cover misread apostrophes in contractions, mid-word capital X, V, and I, optional ellipsis folding, and bogus italic runs around single characters and punctuation. An unread glyph now costs nothing when matching a word against the dictionary, so "batt□e" resolves to "battle" and a damaged name can be restored from the item's metadata. A new "Reprocess extracted subtitles" task re-OCRs every logged file from its source with the current settings. Each file is probed with a single ffprobe pass rather than two, and only one OCR or reprocess task runs at a time.

Detect image-based subtitles by probing every Movie and Episode with ffprobe, since Jellyfin does not store PGS/VobSub streams in its metadata. A per-file cache skips files that are unchanged and were probed within a configurable window, with a force-rescan option. Word spacing now scales with subtitle line height, fixing spaces inserted between every letter on HD Blu-ray text. New options: extract only selected languages, discard a track whose dropped-event ratio is too high (removing any stale SRT), and a filename tag ({name}.{lang}.OCR.srt, shown as the subtitle title) so the plugin only ever overwrites or deletes its own output. A config-page modal lists every extracted file with a link to its movie or episode. The scheduled task now sits under its own category.

Detect image-based subtitles by probing every Movie and Episode with ffprobe, since Jellyfin does not store PGS/VobSub streams in its metadata. A per-file cache skips files that are unchanged and were probed within a configurable window, with a force-rescan option. A config-page modal lists every extracted file with a link to its movie or episode. The scheduled task now sits under its own category.

Initial production release. OCRs embedded image-based subtitles (VobSub/DVD and PGS/Blu-ray) to SRT files using nOCR pattern matching, with Subtitle Edit-compatible .nocr databases. Pure managed code: no Tesseract, no native dependencies. Optionally skips image tracks whose language already has a text subtitle.
